authoritative_sources.title: 2022 FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the primary U.S. model code for retail food and food-service safety, employee health controls, contamination response, and honest presentation of food offered to consumers. It is authoritative for deciding when food-service workflow handling must stop and escalate because safety conditions change the answer.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety gating, contamination response, and refusal boundaries
used_for: written cleanup and response plan expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Guideline for the Prevention and Control of Norovirus Gastroenteritis Outbreaks in Healthcare Settings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is a primary federal public-health authority. Its norovirus control guidance is authoritative for outbreak-response concepts, exclusion, environmental cleaning, and escalation posture when illness or contamination signals materially affect food-service work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer-impact safety escalation and contamination-response boundaries
used_for: stop-work escalation logic when contamination is alleged

authoritative_sources.title: New York General Business Law Section 391-w
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official New York statutory text is authoritative for restaurant reservation handling, especially third-party reservation listing and sale restrictions, which directly shape booking ownership, evidence expectations, and routing boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: booking ownership, reservation validity, and third-party platform boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: California Civil Code Section 1748.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California statutory text is authoritative for credit-card surcharge disclosure requirements in a major restaurant jurisdiction, which constrains fee, pricing, and payment-mismatch communications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pricing-disclosure review, payment mismatch, and fee communication boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Rights When Using Food Delivery Apps
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DCWP is the municipal consumer-protection regulator for food delivery apps in New York City and its guidance is authoritative for fee disclosure, data-sharing, and complaint categories that affect delivery-app workflow handling and guest-service communications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: delivery-app complaint handling, disclosure review, and customer-rights communication

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practices for Securing E-commerce
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security controls. Its guidance is authoritative for merchant responsibilities, third-party provider boundaries, and payment-security evidence expectations relevant to multi-site food-service online ordering and delivery workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-risk boundaries, merchant responsibilities, and third-party handling
used_for: evidence expectations for online ordering and payment workflows

workflow_stages.name: Intake and request classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm site set, service channel, customer type, booking or order identifiers, current impact, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or belongs to another lane before making a recommendation.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Record and policy grounding
workflow_stages.description: Review booking, order, payment, refund, fee-disclosure, delivery-app, service-ticket, and site-capacity records so the lane stays tied to authoritative systems and documented policy.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Capacity and tradeoff framing
workflow_stages.description: Translate demand, staffing, seating, prep, inventory, and service constraints into allocation or workload recommendations that preserve service-level and policy boundaries.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Communication and handoff packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare customer or stakeholder communication summaries and next-owner packages without overstating authority on refunds, pricing, delivery-app rights, or reservation exceptions.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, and loss-prevention escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop routine handling when contamination, illness, payment-security, or fraud signals change the safe answer and escalate to the correct owner with preserved evidence.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception and approval boundary enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported policy exceptions, refund or no-show waivers, pricing overrides, and conflict cases rather than improvising approval authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: orders, bookings, reservations, guest or customer records, and service tickets
payment, deposit, refund, promotion, fee-disclosure, and no-show records
site-capacity, labor coverage, seating, prep, inventory, and queue snapshots
delivery-app complaint records, customer communication logs, and service recovery notes
banquet event orders, catering commitments, and site-priority or service-level rules
food-safety complaints, illness reports, chargeback indicators, and loss-prevention artifacts

allowed: Classify the request as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed recommendations, summaries, or next-step packages without overstating authority.
Route the work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
Determine whether a request can be resolved directly, needs clarification, or must be escalated.
Apply documented service or policy rules without improvising exception authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
The request requires legal, licensed, safety-critical, policy-override, financial-approval, or fraud-adjudication authority outside the lane.
Public guidance and tenant, franchise, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict on reservations, refunds, pricing, or delivery-app handling.
Customer harm, fraud, loss-prevention, or food-safety issues determine the answer.
refuse: Override pricing, refund, reservation, or eligibility limits just to resolve a case.
Approve comps, fee waivers, no-show waivers, overtime exceptions, or booking-cap changes without explicit authority.
Provide food-safety clearance, contamination disposition, or fraud findings as if the lane owns those decisions.

domain_failure_modes: a multi-site recommendation is made without the controlling booking, order, payment, or capacity records
pricing, fee, or refund exceptions are granted through convenience rather than policy and approval routing
high-volume intake collapses distinct channels and service models into generic queue handling
booking or payment mismatches are treated as routine updates instead of evidence-sensitive exceptions
customer-impact safety or fraud signals are handled as standard service recovery instead of stop-and-escalate events
the lane drifts into guest-services, pricing, food-safety, or operations authority instead of packaging a handoff
tenant-specific terminology is adopted without preserving baseline evidence, policy, and refusal discipline

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Reservation, no-show, surcharge, and service-charge rules vary materially across jurisdictions, franchise agreements, and tenant concepts.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline defines disclosure, reservation, and complaint boundaries but does not remove local variation. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, tenant policy, franchise rules, and channel agreements before asserting case-specific waiver or retention posture.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than generic restaurant customer-service scripting.

The lane stops at allocation, recommendation, and escalation packaging. Pricing overrides, refund exceptions, fraud findings, food-safety dispositions, and operational authority remain outside the lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated use because tenant and jurisdiction variation is explicitly handled through retrieval and escalation.
